quiz.title: Grade-10 Physics: Unit 6 Quiz 11
quiz.subtitle: Simple Harmonic Motion — Introduction
quiz.subject: Physics
quiz.topic: Grade 10 - Waves and Simple Harmonic Motion

questions.question: In simple harmonic motion, the restoring force always acts:
options: In the direction of the velocity
questions.explanation: The restoring force always points toward the equilibrium position, opposite to the displacement.

questions.question: At the equilibrium position, the displacement of the oscillating object is:
questions.explanation: The equilibrium position is the point of zero displacement — the object's rest position.

questions.question: At the point of maximum displacement in SHM, the speed of the object is:
options: Equal to half the maximum speed
questions.explanation: At the extreme positions, the object momentarily stops before reversing direction. Speed is zero.

questions.question: As the oscillating object passes through the equilibrium position, its speed is:
questions.explanation: The object moves fastest through equilibrium because the restoring force has been accelerating it toward that point.

questions.question: At the point of maximum displacement, the acceleration of the object is:
questions.explanation: Acceleration is greatest at the extremes because the restoring force is largest there ($F = -kx$).

questions.question: The amplitude of an oscillation is the:
questions.explanation: Amplitude is the maximum distance the object moves from its equilibrium position.

questions.question: One complete oscillation in SHM is:
options: Motion from equilibrium to maximum displacement only
Motion from one extreme to the other and back
The total distance traveled in one second
questions.explanation: A complete oscillation means the object returns to its starting position with the same velocity — a full cycle.

questions.question: The period of an oscillation is the:
options: Time taken for one complete cycle
Force required to stretch a spring
questions.explanation: Period ($T$) is the time for one complete oscillation, measured in seconds.

questions.question: The frequency of an oscillation is the:
options: Number of complete cycles per second
Distance from equilibrium to the extreme
questions.explanation: Frequency ($f$) is the number of oscillations per second, measured in hertz (Hz).

questions.question: In ideal SHM with no friction or air resistance, the total mechanical energy:
questions.explanation: Without energy loss, energy continuously converts between kinetic and potential forms, but the total stays constant.

scoreMessages.90: Excellent! You've mastered the fundamentals of simple harmonic motion!
scoreMessages.70: Good work! Review the relationship between displacement, speed, and acceleration in SHM.
scoreMessages.50: Keep practicing! Focus on how speed and acceleration vary at equilibrium vs. extreme positions.
scoreMessages.0: Review the basics: restoring force direction, equilibrium, amplitude, period, and frequency.
